blockingIssues.description: `testUpgradePreservesRotatingKVCacheState` still gates its meaningful cache-preservation assertions behind a transient `scheduler.currentState == "batched"` snapshot and otherwise falls back to token-only checks, so the broken pre-fix migration path could still pass.

Scheduler prompt-cache write-back still stores finished KV caches under the pre-generation `inputTokens` key even though the stored cache has advanced through generated tokens, so repeated prompts and ChatSession follow-ups can retrieve a cache whose depth does not match the matched trie key.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen scheduler-regression test guidance so workers must make upgrade/timing assertions deterministic: avoid gating critical checks on transient `InferenceScheduler.currentState` snapshots, and for prompt-time fixes assert meaningful lower bounds or use controlled delays instead of only `promptTime > 0`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `fix-rotating-cache-test-vacuous` found the new cache assertions only run while a transient `.batched` actor state is still visible, and the review for `fix-joiner-prompt-time-and-metadata` found the new timing regression test only asserts `promptTime > 0` even though the prior bug was specifically near-zero latency for 3rd+ joiners.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require prompt-cache write-back fixes to prove end-to-end reuse of the just-written cache on a second identical request or ChatSession turn, not merely that an entry was inserted into `LRUPromptCache`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `fix-prompt-cache-wiring-completeness` found the new tests stop at insertion assertions, which allowed a key/cache-depth mismatch in scheduler write-back to persist even though cache entries were present.
